title: SQL Injection: Login Bypass
description: A login endpoint is vulnerable to SQL injection. Your job is to fix the login() function using parameterized queries so that SQL injection attacks are blocked while normal login still works.
hint: Use cursor.execute() with ? placeholders instead of string formatting.
canonical_fix: cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = ? AND password = ?', (username, password))
explanation: String concatenation in SQL queries allows attackers to manipulate the query logic. Parameterized queries treat user input as data, never as SQL code.

attack_flow.label: Query built
attack_flow.value: SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '' OR '1'='1' --' AND password = '...'

attack_flow.label: SQL parsed
attack_flow.value: WHERE is always TRUE (1=1); password check commented out with --

attack_flow.label: Result
attack_flow.value: Authentication bypassed — logged in without valid credentials

why_blocked: Parameterized queries (`?` placeholders) pass user input to the database driver as a bound value, separate from the SQL statement. The driver escapes single quotes, so injected syntax like `' OR 1=1--` becomes harmless literal text that matches no username.
